rationale: My round-1 boundary blocker is fixed at 389fcc41. TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks now PASSES (verified: go test ./internal/lint/ -run TestEnforcementHasNoGitLeaks -v -> PASS, both subtests). go list -f '{{.Imports}}' ./internal/approve | grep gitutil is empty: internal/approve no longer imports internal/gitutil. The reroute (git diff 21374bb8..389fcc41, 2 files) is a clean, seam-transparent fix: new internal/lifecycle/gitquery.go adds thin exported wrappers IsAncestor and BranchExists delegating to gitutil (lifecycle is not an ADR-0030 enforcement package, so this is a legal boundary crossing); internal/approve/impl.go repoints implIsAncestorFn and implBranchExistsFn to the lifecycle wrappers with identical names/signatures, so all AC seam tests are unaffected. No import cycle (lifecycle has zero dependency back on approve). Ran the CI-exact gates at 389fcc41: go build ./... clean, gofmt -l ./cmd ./internal empty, go vet ./... clean, go test -short ./... fails ONLY internal/instruct.TestRun_IdleNoBeads (z4ps, worktree-only artifact -- this worktree has an active spec), golangci-lint run ./... reports ONLY the 2 pre-existing gosec G115 in internal/journal/lock_unix.go (8ud6). Confirmed both pre-existing via git diff origin/main -- internal/journal/ and internal/gitutil/ (both empty -- untouched by spec 115). No BranchExistsE/show-ref hits in internal/approve. No new spec-115 CI failure. Flipping to APPROVE.
